Title: Antivirus isoindolinone alkaloids with rare oxocyclopenta[f]isoindole frameworks isolated from the stems of flue-cured tobacco

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtHsp70 protein accumulation increased in TMV-inoculated tobacco leaves compared with healthy leaves. 2. Pretreatment with compounds 1, 3, and 4 significantly reduced NtHsp70 protein accumulation on day 7 after TMV inoculation. 3. The authors suggest suppression of NtHsp70 expression is associated with inhibition of TMV infection.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced NtHsp70 protein accumulation is associated with reduced TMV infection/accumulation in tobacco.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein level higher in TMV-inoculated tobacco than in healthy tobacco leaves.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein accumulation analyzed in inoculated tobacco leaves on day 7 after foliar spraying and TMV inoculation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Western blot analysis of NtHsp70 protein in TMV-inoculated tobacco leaves after compound pretreatment.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TMV inoculation; foliar spraying with 20 μM compounds 1, 3, or 4; sampled on day 7.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper is a natural-products pharmacology study, not a gene-function study in nutrient metabolism. NtHsp70 is discussed as a host factor related to antiviral resistance rather than nutrient metabolism.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Accumulation of TMV-CP in inoculated leaves was lower in plants treated with compounds 1, 3, and 4 than in negative control plants. 2. TMV-CP transcript levels in newly grown systemic leaves on day 7 were also reduced by compounds 1, 3, and 4. 3. Reduced TMV-CP accumulation was used as evidence for antiviral activity and SAR induction.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced TMV-CP transcript/protein accumulation indicates inhibition of TMV infection in tobacco.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Accumulates after TMV infection; lower in compound-treated leaves.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in inoculated leaves on days 3 and 7 and in systemic leaves on day 7 after TMV inoculation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Western blot analysis of TMV-CP protein and semi-quantitative RT-PCR of TMV-CP transcripts.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TMV inoculation; foliar spraying with 20 μM compounds 1, 3, or 4.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TMV-CP is a viral marker used to monitor antiviral efficacy, not a plant nutrient metabolism gene.
